name: AREthSendARPDUTo
title: AREthSendARPDUTo
syntax: long AREthSendARPDUTo( dword aep, dword dstIPv4Addr, dword dstPort, dword bufferLength, char buffer[], dword headerID ); // form 1
long AREthSendARPDUTo( dword aep, dword dstIPv4Addr, dword dstPort, dword bufferLength, byte buffer[], dword headerID ); // form 2
long AREthSendARPDUTo( dword aep, dword dstIPv4Addr, dword dstPort, dword bufferLength, struct buffer, dword headerID ); // form 3
long AREthSendARPDUTo( dword aep, byte dstIPv6Addr[], dword dstPort, dword bufferLength, char buffer[], dword headerID ); // form 4
long AREthSendARPDUTo( dword aep, byte dstIPv6Addr[], dword dstPort, dword bufferLength, byte buffer[], dword headerID ); // form 5
long AREthSendARPDUTo( dword aep, byte dstIPv6Addr[], dword dstPort, dword bufferLength, struct buffer, dword headerID ); // form 6
long AREthSendARPDUTo( dword aep, IP_Endpoint dstIPEndpoint, dword bufferLength, char buffer[], dword headerID ); // form 7
long AREthSendARPDUTo( dword aep, IP_Endpoint dstIPEndpoint, dword bufferLength, byte buffer[], dword headerID ); // form 8
long AREthSendARPDUTo( dword aep, IP_Endpoint dstIPEndpoint, dword bufferLength, struct buffer, dword headerID ); // form 9
description: Transmits data as AUTOSAR PDU via an Ethernet connection with activated Header option. Before transmitting each AUTOSAR PDU gets an Header that contains an ID and length.

parameters.name: aep
parameters.description: Handle of an application endpoint that was opened with AREthOpenLocalApplicationEndpoint.

parameters.name: dstIPv4Addr
parameters.description: IPv4 address of the target as dword, e.g. converted with ipGetAddressAsNumber.

parameters.name: dstIPv6Addr
parameters.description: IPv6 address of the target as byte array, e.g. converted with ipGetAddressAsArray.

parameters.name: dstPort
parameters.description: UDP or TCP port of the target.

parameters.name: dstIPEndpoint
parameters.description: Object of type IP_Endpoint that contains the address/port of the target.

parameters.name: bufferLength
parameters.description: Buffer length of the data. The length is written in the preceded header during transmission.

parameters.name: headerId
parameters.description: ID which is to be written in the header.

availability: 9.0 SP2: form 1-3 10.0 SP4: form 4-6 12.0 SP2: form 7-9
2.1 SP3: form 1-3 2.2 SP4: form 4-6 4.0 SP2: form 7-9
